```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: 'asis'

csv_name <- "related_posts.csv"
# Check paths (relative to the post in /posts/)
if (file.exists(csv_name)) {
    csv_path <- csv_name
} else if (file.exists(paste0("../", csv_name))) {
    csv_path <- paste0("../", csv_name)
} else {
    csv_path <- NULL
}

if (!is.null(csv_path)) {
    # Read CSV
    df <- read.csv(csv_path, row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)

    # 1. Clean the current filename (remove .qmd or .rmarkdown)
    current_file_raw <- basename(knitr::current_input())
    current_key <- gsub("\\.(qmd|rmarkdown|md)$", "", current_file_raw)

    # 2. Clean the CSV column/row names for matching
    # We create a mapping so we can find the original filename later
    clean_cols <- gsub("\\.(qmd|rmarkdown|md)$", "", colnames(df))
    colnames(df) <- clean_cols
    rownames(df) <- clean_cols

    # 3. Match and Display
    if (current_key %in% colnames(df)) {
        sims <- df[, current_key]
        names(sims) <- rownames(df)

        # Exclude self and filter low scores
        sims <- sims[names(sims) != current_key]
        sims <- sims[sims > 0.01]

        if (length(sims) > 0) {
            top_n <- min(5, length(sims))
            top_5 <- sort(sims, decreasing = TRUE)[1:top_n]

            cat("\n### সম্পর্কিত লেখা (Related Posts)\n\n")
            for (i in seq_along(top_5)) {
                # Get the name and format it for the link
                # Since we stripped extensions, we add .html for the link
                # because Quarto converts .qmd to .html in the final site
                slug <- names(top_5)[i]

                display_name <- gsub("-", " ", slug)
                display_name <- paste0(toupper(substr(display_name, 1, 1)), substr(display_name, 2, nchar(display_name)))

                cat(paste0("- [", display_name, "](", slug, ".html)\n"))
            }
        }
    }
}
```
